prompt: Operator: ApplyAdam in-place (non-Nesterov), float32. Inputs var,m,v,grad + 6 host scalars folded into ConstExpr floats (one_minus_beta1, one_minus_beta2, alpha, epsilon). Per element: m_new=m+(grad-m)*one_minus_beta1; v_new=v+(grad*grad-v)*one_minus_beta2; var_new=var-(m_new*alpha)/(sqrt(v_new)+epsilon) (or the algebraically-equivalent (m_new*alpha)*rsqrt(v_new) -- dropping the +eps OUTSIDE the sqrt costs ~alpha*m*eps/v ~ 1e-12, far under atol 1e-5). Store m_new,v_new,var_new in place. Input shapes: [16,2048],[32,4096]; perf shape [32,4096]. Flatten to 1D; TILE_SIZE=2048, CORE_NUM=16, size multiple of 32768 (aligned_only); partition tile_per_core via `asc2.range(tile_per_block, unroll_factor=1, parallel=True)`. HONEST PERF NOTE: this op is memory-bound (4 loads + 3 stores/elem f32); a copy-only diagnostic of the same tensors floors at ~17000 ticks across ALL tile sizes, ~2.1x the hand-written aclnnApplyAdam (8107). Do NOT expect to beat 0.70 -- double-buffer (unroll=2) overflows UB at TILE=2048 and gives no gain at TILE=1024; the goal is a correct vector-only realization, not a perf win. Tolerance atol=1e-5, rtol=1e-4 vs NumPy. Reference identity: pure ApplyAdamD has no public aclnn; aclnnApplyAdam (ascend950) is the canonical baseline. Platform Ascend950PR_9599. Build/run: python3.11 kernel.py -r Model -v Ascend950PR_9599. Workaround: Reference golden/kernels/apply_adam_f32.py for the proven in-place 4-load/3-store structure.
